methods.description: This method describes the process of analyzing the hydrogen isotopic composition of alkenones using a Thermo Finnigan DELTA Plus XL GC/TC/irMS. It is used to determine isotopic values for alkenones, which are standardized against reference gases and monitored for precision using internal standards.

steps.description: The temperature conditions of the GC increased from 70 to 145°C at 20°C min−1, then to 320°C at 4°C min−1, at which it was held isothermal for 13 min using an Agilent CP Sil-5 column.
steps.searchable_summary: Set GC temperature conditions for alkenone analysis

steps.description: The thermal conversion temperature was set to 1425°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Set thermal conversion temperature for isotope analysis

steps.description: The H+3 correction factor was determined daily and ranged between 10 and 14.
Isotopic values for alkenones were standardized against pulses of H2 reference gas, injected three times at the beginning and two times at the end of each run.
steps.searchable_summary: Standardize isotopic values using H2 reference gas

steps.description: A set of standard n-alkanes with known isotopic composition was analyzed daily prior to each sample batch to monitor system performance.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze standard n-alkanes to monitor system performance

steps.description: Samples were only analyzed when the alkanes in Mix B had an average deviation from their off-line determined value of < 5 ‰.
steps.searchable_summary: Ensure sample analysis only when deviation is < 5 ‰

steps.description: Squalane was co-injected as an internal standard with each sample to monitor the precision of the alkenone isotope values.
steps.searchable_summary: Co-inject squalane as internal standard for precision monitoring

steps.description: Alkenone δD values were measured as the combined peak of the C37:2 and C37:3 alkenones, and fractions were analyzed in duplicates if sufficient sample material was available.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure δD values of alkenones and analyze in duplicates

methods.description: This method outlines the analysis of TEX H 86 using an Agilent 1100 series HPLC/MS. It involves separation and detection of GDGTs, followed by calculation of TEX H 86 values and absolute temperatures for temperature reconstruction.

steps.description: Separation was achieved on an Alltech Prevail Cyano column, maintained at 30°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate GDGTs using HPLC on Cyano column

steps.description: GDGTs were eluted with 99% hexane and 1% propanol for 5 min, followed by a linear gradient to 1.8% propanol in 45 min, followed by backflushing hexane/propanol at 0.2 mL min−1 for 10 min.
Detection was achieved using atmospheric pressure positive ion chemical ionization mass spectrometry (APCI-MS) of the eluent.
GDGTs were detected by single ion monitoring (SIM) of their [M + H]+ ions and quantified by integration of the peak areas.
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify GDGTs using SIM and peak integration

steps.description: The TEX H 86 values and absolute temperatures were calculated according to Kim et al. (2010).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate TEX H 86 values and temperatures
